reasoning: There is no evidence that an actual blockade of a Taiwanese seaport by China has occurred or that the US has accused them of one up to May 6, 2025. Chinese military drills (such as Strait Thunder 2025A in April 2025) involve 'simulating' or 'rehearsing' blockades, which the US has condemned, but explicitly characterized as exercises or rehearsals. A true blockade would be an unprecedented act of war or major conflict. With less than two months until the July 1, 2025 deadline, the base rate likelihood of such a massive geopolitical conflict spontaneously erupting and leading to formal US blockade accusations is extremely low (< 2%).
